Vector Algebra.q: Â is a vector quantity such that |Â| = non-zero constant. Which of the following expression is true for Â?
Vector Algebra.explanation: Cross product of any vector with itself is always zero: Â × Â = |A||A|sin(0°) = 0. But dot product Â · Â = |A|² ≠ 0 since |A| is non-zero.

Vector Algebra.q: The velocity of a particle is given as v̂ = -xî + 2yĵ - zk̂ m/s. The magnitude of acceleration at point (1, 2, 4) is _____ m/s².
The angle between vector Q̂ and the resultant of (2Q̂ + 2P̂) and (2Q̂ - 2P̂) is:
Vector Algebra.explanation: Resultant of (2Q̂+2P̂) and (2Q̂-2P̂) = 4Q̂. Since 4Q̂ is in same direction as Q̂, angle between them = 0°.

Vector Algebra.q: If two vectors Â and B̂ having equal magnitude R are inclined at angle θ, then:
Vector Algebra.explanation: |Â + B̂| = √(R²+R²+2R²cosθ) = R√(2+2cosθ) = R√(4cos²(θ/2)) = 2R cos(θ/2)

Vector Algebra.q: A vector in x-y plane makes an angle of 30° with y-axis. The magnitude of y-component of vector is 2√3. The magnitude of x-component of the vector will be:
Vector Algebra.explanation: y-component = R cos30° = 2√3. So R = 4. x-component = R sin30° = 4 × 0.5 = 2

Vector Algebra.q: When vector Â = 2î + 3ĵ + 2k̂ is subtracted from vector B̂, it gives a vector equal to 2ĵ. Then the magnitude of vector B̂ will be:
Two forces having magnitude A and A/2 are perpendicular to each other. The magnitude of their resultant is:
If two vectors P̂ = î + 2mĵ + mk̂ and Q̂ = 4î - 2ĵ + mk̂ are perpendicular to each other, the value of m will be:
Vector Algebra.explanation: For perpendicular vectors P̂·Q̂ = 0. (1)(4)+(2m)(-2)+(m)(m) = 0 → 4-4m+m² = 0 → (m-2)² = 0 → m = 2

Vector Algebra.q: Two vectors Â and B̂ have equal magnitudes. If magnitude of Â + B̂ is equal to two times the magnitude of Â - B̂, then the angle between Â and B̂ will be:
Vector Algebra.explanation: |Â+B̂| = 2|Â-B̂|. Let |A|=|B|=R. 2R²(1+cosθ) = 4·2R²(1-cosθ). Solving: 5cosθ = 3 → θ = cos⁻¹(3/5)

Vector Algebra.q: Which of the following relations is true for two unit vectors Â and B̂ making an angle θ to each other?
Vector Algebra.explanation: For unit vectors: |Â+B̂| = 2cos(θ/2) and |Â-B̂| = 2sin(θ/2). So |Â-B̂|/|Â+B̂| = tan(θ/2). Therefore |Â-B̂| = |Â+B̂| tan(θ/2)

Vector Algebra.q:
Statement I: Two forces (P̂+Q̂) and (P̂-Q̂) where P̂⊥Q̂, when act at angle θ₁ their resultant is √3(P²+Q²). When they act at angle θ₂ the resultant becomes √2(P²+Q²). This is possible only when θ₁ < θ₂.
Statement II: In the above situation θ₁ = 60° and θ₂ = 90°.
Choose the most appropriate answer:
options: A) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
B) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
C) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
D) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Vector Algebra.explanation: At θ₁=60° resultant = √3(P²+Q²) and at θ₂=90° resultant = √2(P²+Q²). Since 60°<90°, θ₁<θ₂. Both statements are true.

Vector Algebra.q: Two vectors X̂ and Ŷ have equal magnitude. The magnitude of (X̂ - Ŷ) is n times the magnitude of (X̂ + Ŷ). The angle between X̂ and Ŷ is:
Vector Algebra.explanation: |X̂-Ŷ|² = n²|X̂+Ŷ|². 2R²(1-cosθ) = n²·2R²(1+cosθ). Solving: cosθ = (1-n²)/(1+n²) = (-n²-1)/(n²-1)

Vector Algebra.q: What will be the projection of vector Â = î + ĵ + k̂ on vector B̂ = î + ĵ?
Vector Algebra.explanation: Projection of Â on B̂ = (Â·B̂/|B̂|²)B̂. Â·B̂ = 1+1+0 = 2. |B̂|² = 2. Projection = (2/2)(î+ĵ) = (î+ĵ)

Vector Algebra.q: Two vectors P̂ and Q̂ have equal magnitudes. If the magnitude of P̂ + Q̂ is n times the magnitude of P̂ - Q̂, then angle between P̂ and Q̂ is:
Vector Algebra.explanation: |P̂+Q̂| = n|P̂-Q̂|. 2R²(1+cosθ) = n²·2R²(1-cosθ). cosθ(1+n²) = n²-1. θ = cos⁻¹((n²-1)/(n²+1))

Vector Algebra.q: If Â and B̂ are two vectors satisfying the relation Â·B̂ = |Â×B̂|. Then the value of |Â - B̂| will be:
Vector Algebra.explanation: Â·B̂ = ABcosθ = ABsinθ = |Â×B̂|. So tanθ = 1, θ = 45°. |Â-B̂|² = A²+B²-2ABcos45° = A²+B²-√2·AB

Vector Algebra.q: Let |A̋₁| = 3, |A̋₂| = 5 and |A̋₁ + A̋₂| = 5. The value of (2A̋₁ + 3A̋₂)·(3A̋₁ - 2A̋₂) is:
Two vectors Â and B̂ have equal magnitudes. The magnitude of (Â + B̂) is 'n' times the magnitude of (Â - B̂). The angle between Â and B̂ is:
Vector Algebra.explanation: |Â+B̂| = n|Â-B̂|. 2R²(1+cosθ) = n²·2R²(1-cosθ). cosθ = (n²-1)/(n²+1). θ = cos⁻¹[(n²-1)/(n²+1)]

Vector Algebra.q: Let Â = (î + ĵ) and B̂ = (2î - ĵ). The magnitude of a coplanar vector Ĉ such that Â·Ĉ = B̂·Ĉ = Â·B̂ is given by:
Two particles are at equal distance from origin with position vectors Â = 2î + 3nĵ + 2k̂ and B̂ = 2î - 2ĵ + 4pk̂. If both vectors are perpendicular to each other, the value of n⁻¹ is ___. (Numerical)
Vector Algebra.explanation: Â·B̂ = 0. (2)(2)+(3n)(-2)+(2)(4p) = 0 → 4-6n+8p=0. Also |Â|=|B̂|: 9n²=16p². Solving these gives n⁻¹.

Vector Algebra.q: The resultant of two vectors Â and B̂ is perpendicular to Â and its magnitude is half that of B̂. The angle between vectors Â and B̂ is ___°. (Numerical)
Vector Algebra.explanation: Let angle = θ. Resultant R⊥Â. Using vector triangle with sine rule: sin(angle between R and B̂) = |Â|/|B̂|. |R|=|B̂|/2. Working gives θ = 150°.
